message:
fix(abi): translate managed pthread attrs to host attrs in pthread_create, validate barrier attrs

- Add managed_attr_data_for_host_translation() to detect managed
  PthreadAttrData overlays vs raw host attrs, rejecting zeroed-out
  (destroyed/uninitialized) attrs as EINVAL.
- Add host_pthread_create_with_managed_attr() which rebuilds a native
  host pthread_attr_t from the managed overlay fields (detach state,
  stack size/addr, guard size, inherit sched, sched policy/priority,
  scope, CPU affinity via extended registry, sigmask via
  pthread_attr_setsigmask_np) before delegating to the real host
  pthread_create.
- Wire the new translation path into the public pthread_create export
  so managed attrs are faithfully forwarded to glibc instead of being
  passed as opaque bytes.
- Add attr validation to pthread_barrier_init: reject null-magic
  (destroyed) barrierattr and PTHREAD_PROCESS_SHARED pshared value.
- Add tests: barrier_init_rejects_destroyed_attr,
  barrier_init_rejects_process_shared_attr,
  thread_create_with_managed_attr_succeeds,
  thread_create_rejects_destroyed_managed_attr.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
